rationale: The cited paper is used in the hypothesis-development section to justify a specific behavioral mechanism, not merely as broad background. The authors invoke Park, Jun, and MacInnis (2000), alongside loss-aversion work, to explain asymmetry between switching up versus down in option configurations and to motivate H6 about base versus advanced defaults. That mechanism is then carried into the citing paper's empirical model and tests of mass-customization design choices. Even though the citation appears in a small group, its role is functionally tied to a focal hypothesis.
relation_description: The citing paper transfers the cited paper's decision-framing and asymmetric loss-aversion insight to a new context: default design in mass-customized PC configuration. It uses that prior logic to predict and test that a base default can yield higher achieved product utility than an advanced default.
